content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all three pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation correctness. Amendment 1: Changed the dedup key in reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings from a 2-tuple (finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) to a 3-tuple (active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id), matching the canonical identity key used in rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal. This prevents cross-bead suppression where two different active beads with the same finding mapped to the same target bead would incorrectly be deduplicated. Amendment 2: Added an optional run_id field to PlannedElsewhereMapping with serde(default, skip_serializing_if) for backward compatibility. The engine's record_planned_elsewhere_amendments now accepts run_id and stamps it on each mapping. The reconstruction path in success_reconciliation stamps run_id on reconstructed mappings. The journal metadata includes run_id when present, and rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal extracts it. verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success filters loaded mappings by run_id using is_none_or for backward-compatible None handling, so stale mappings from rolled-back or abandoned runs are not re-verified or commented. Amendment 3: Added an Unknown variant with #[serde(other)] to MilestoneEventType, allowing older code (or rollback/downgrade scenarios) to deserialize journal files containing planned_elsewhere_mapped events without failing with CorruptRecord. The Unknown variant is a unit variant that acts as a catch-all for any unrecognized event type string. All existing consumers use equality checks (not exhaustive matches) so no code changes were needed beyond the enum definition.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: Changed the dedup key in reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings (success_reconciliation.rs) from HashSet<(String, String)> keyed by (finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) to HashSet<(String, String, String)> keyed by (active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id). Updated both the initial population from existing_mappings and the insert after each reconstruction.
2. [Completed] Amendment 2a: Added run_id: Option<String> field to PlannedElsewhereMapping in model.rs with #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] for backward-compatible serialization.
3. [Completed] Amendment 2b: Added run_id parameter to record_planned_elsewhere_amendments in engine.rs and threaded it from both call sites (Complete path and Restart path) where run_id is available in scope. The mapping construction now includes run_id: Some(run_id.as_str().to_owned()).
4. [Completed] Amendment 2c: Added run_id to journal metadata in record_planned_elsewhere_mapping (service.rs) when present, and extraction in rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal for round-trip persistence.
5. [Completed] Amendment 2d: Added run_id filtering in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success — bead_mappings filter now checks m.run_id.as_deref().is_none_or(|rid| rid == run_id) so only current-run mappings (or legacy None mappings) are processed.
6. [Completed] Amendment 2e: Updated all PlannedElsewhereMapping construction sites: planned_elsewhere.rs (run_id: None), success_reconciliation.rs verified mapping (inherits from outcome), reconstruction (run_id: Some(run_id)), and 6 test construction sites in service.rs (run_id: None).
7. [Completed] Amendment 3: Added Unknown variant with #[serde(other)] attribute to MilestoneEventType enum in model.rs. This catch-all allows any unrecognized event type string to deserialize as Unknown instead of failing, providing forward compatibility for journal files written by newer code versions.
8. [Completed] Fixed clippy warning: changed map_or(true, |rid| rid == run_id) to is_none_or(|rid| rid == run_id) per clippy::unnecessary_map_or suggestion.
9. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt to fix one minor formatting issue (multi-line filter closure collapsed to single line).
10. [Completed] Ran cargo test (1617 passed: 684 lib + 174 integration + 1 tmux + 758 unit, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 1617 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (684 lib, 174 integration, 1 tmux, 758 unit, 9 doc-tests ignored)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (success_reconciliation.rs, planned_elsewhere.rs, model.rs, service.rs, engine.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- Amendment 1 verified: seen_keys in reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings is now HashSet<(String, String, String)> with 3-tuple keys matching the canonical identity used in rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal. Two different active beads with the same finding/target pair are no longer cross-suppressed.
- Amendment 2 verified: PlannedElsewhereMapping.run_id is Option<String> with serde(default) for backward compatibility. Engine stamps run_id on production mappings. Journal metadata includes run_id when present. Rebuild extracts it. verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success filters by run_id using is_none_or for backward-compatible None handling.
- Amendment 3 verified: MilestoneEventType now has Unknown variant with #[serde(other)]. Any journal line with an unrecognized event_type deserializes as Unknown instead of causing CorruptRecord. No exhaustive matches exist on MilestoneEventType — all consumers use equality checks that naturally skip Unknown.
- Files modified: src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs (run_id field + Unknown variant), src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs (run_id in journal metadata + extraction + test sites), src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs (3-tuple dedup + run_id on reconstruction + run_id filter), src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs (run_id: None), src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs (run_id parameter + stamping)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- The br_show_error_is_missing pattern for stale-bead detection is duplicated between cli/run.rs and planned_elsewhere.rs; a future consolidation could extract this into a shared utility on BrError.
- With the split verification/commenting approach, if a crash occurs after persisting verified=true but before posting the comment, the comment is permanently lost. This is an accepted tradeoff since comments are best-effort and the alternative (duplicate comments on replay) is worse.
- The reconcile_planned_elsewhere function (used only in tests, not production orchestration) does not have run_id context and stamps run_id: None on its mappings. This is acceptable because the production path through record_planned_elsewhere_amendments in engine.rs always provides run_id, and the backward-compatible filter in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success treats None as matching any run.
- The #[serde(other)] catch-all on MilestoneEventType means that if a future version adds a new event type and then rolls back, the Unknown variant will silently absorb those events during deserialization. Consumers that iterate journal events should be aware that Unknown events may appear and should skip them.
